channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: A Panhandle town says goodbye to its newspaper
item.description: Watch this documentary about the final run of the Canadian Record, a small-town, family-owned newspaper that closed last year.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Texas Tribune Staff, KPRC 2

item.title: Supreme Court races in key states could tip scales on policy questions
item.description: Unlike Georgia and Nevada, battleground states such as Arizona, North Carolina and Michigan will likely see high-stakes state Supreme Court races.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: As a cautious Kamala loses momentum, Democrats are panicking over a Trump win
item.description: What seemed to be an unstoppable hype train for the Kamala Harris campaign is now screeching to a halt – and the polls show it all too clearly.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Eric Adams is 'working well through the chaos': Gov. Hochul
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Gov. Kathy Hochul offered support for embattled New York City Mayor Eric Adams during the Columbus Day Parade in Manhattan on Monday. "Told him to bring in new blood… he's doing that… working well through the chaos," she told PIX11 News.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Election 2024: Races to watch in New Jersey
item.description: NEW JERSEY (PX11) -- It's been one of the most notable years for New Jersey politics in recent memory — indictments, upsets, and loss are just some themes of what has been a year that could shape the Garden State's political future for years to come.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vasil, PIX 11

item.title: Voters with disabilities are feeling ignored by presidential candidates
item.description: A new report from Rutgers University estimates that about 40.2 million eligible voters in the quickly approaching U.S. presidential election are disabled.
item.dc:creator: KENYA HUNTER and AYANNA ALEXANDER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Control of state House could hinge on a sleepy district outside Duluth
item.description: Control of the Minnesota House of Representatives could come down to one race in a district outside Duluth, where first-term Republican Natalie Zeleznikar is being challenged by Democratic retired judge Mark Munger.
item.dc:creator: Dan Kraker, MPR News

item.title: Johnson: Additional hurricane aid 'can wait' until Congress is back in session
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) on Sunday said passing additional hurricane aid for states impacted by Hurricanes Helene and Milton “can wait” until Congress is back in session after the election.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Tallahassee sends city crews to aid Tampa Bay recovery efforts
item.description: As communities wait for daylight to come Thursday morning to see the damage that has been done and for emergency personnel to start rescue efforts, counties in north Florida are sending their crews to the Tampa Bay area to aid in recovery.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: NJ assemblyman's car stolen, house broken into
item.description: HAZLET, N.J. (PIX11) - Video from the home where a New Jersey assemblyman is currently living shows alleged burglars break into the home and speed away with his personal car - complete with State Assembly plates.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vasil, PIX 11

item.title: Ryan Hamilton discusses homelessness, the Animal Foundation, and F1
item.description: Ryan Hamilton, a Republican candidate for the Clark County Commission, appeared on 8 News Now's Daily Voting Guide to share his perspective on homelessness, animal welfare, and Formula One's impact on the local economy as the election nears.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: The disconnect between economic data and voters' concerns
item.description: Are you worried about money? Concerned about the economy? MPR News host Angela Davis and her guests talk about the financial issues affecting how people plan to vote.
item.dc:creator: Angela Davis and Cari Dwyer, MPR News

item.title: Former Sen. Tim Johnson, the last Democrat to hold statewide office in South Dakota, dies at 77
item.description: Former Sen. Tim Johnson, a centrist who was the last Democrat to hold statewide office in South Dakota and who spent nearly three decades in Congress, has died.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: In battleground states, it's not just about winning — but also losing by less
item.description: In a state where every vote matters, both Democratic and Republican campaigns are not only trying to win in counties where they’re strongest, they’re also trying to lose by less.
item.dc:creator: Ari Shapiro, Mia Venkat, Karen Zamora, and Ashley Brown, MPR News

item.title: Election drop boxes open in San Diego County
item.description: While mail ballots are expected to arrive this week for registered voters, 150 official ballot drop boxes opened Tuesday in San Diego County for the November Presidential General Election.
item.dc:creator: Domenick Candelieri, Fox 5

item.title: Meet the candidates running for Congress in San Diego County
item.description: As Election Day nears, voters in San Diego County will soon get to choose who will represent San Diego County in the U.S. House of Representatives.
item.dc:creator: Anna Ashcraft, Fox 5

item.title: Melania Trump waves off 'hand-swatting incident': A 'mere misunderstanding'
item.description: Melania Trump is waving off speculation that she didn't want to hold her husband's hand, chalking up images of her seemingly "swatting" away former President Donald Trump to a "mere misunderstanding.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, Fox 5
